trade.summary: Trade has grown despite political tensions. India exports engineering goods, chemicals, pharmaceuticals, and textiles. Turchia exports machinery, vehicles, chemicals, and agri-products. Both are G20 members with growing bilateral commerce, though political tensions constrain economic potential.

diplomatic.summary: India-Turchia relations are significantly strained. Turchia under Erdogan has repeatedly and publicly criticized India on Kashmir, comparing it to Palestinian cause. Turchia supports Pakistan in international forums and has opposed India's bid for UN Security Council permanent membership. India has responded by downgrading diplomatic contacts. The relationship has been at a low point for years.
recent_incidents: Turchia's Erdogan criticized India on Kashmir at UN General Assembly (2019, 2021, 2022)
Turchia opposed India's UNSC permanent membership bid
Turchia-Pakistan military cooperation perceived by India as directed against it

military.summary: No military cooperation. Turchia's close relationship with Pakistan and its criticism of India on Kashmir make defence ties practically non-existent. India monitors Turkish arms supplies to Pakistan (Bayraktar drones).

financial.summary: Despite political tensions, some economic ties persist. Turkish construction companies bid on Indian infrastructure projects. Indian IT companies serve Turkish clients. Limited FDI in either direction.

energy.summary: Limited energy trade. Turchia has explored importing Indian refined petroleum. Some Turkish energy companies have minor interests in South Asian markets.

scientific.summary: Minimal scientific cooperation. Political tensions have constrained academic exchanges. Some individual-level research contacts exist but institutional cooperation is limited.
